text: The Fetch API is a modern JavaScript interface for making HTTP requests, replacing XMLHttpRequest. Standardized by WHATWG, it uses Promises for asynchronous request handling and provides a cleaner, more powerful API for network operations.
Key features include: streaming responses via ReadableStream, CORS handling, request/response cloning, and AbortController integration for cancellation.
Unlike XHR, fetch rejects only on network errors — HTTP error statuses (404, 500) still resolve successfully.
